markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Voice of the Elements](/Feats.aspx?ID=8082) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Dragonblood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=794" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Draconic Codex
](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 216

**Prerequisites**
[Primal Dragonblood](/Feats.aspx?ID=5729)

</column>

---

You inherit a primal dragon’s bond to base elements. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392) on creatures with the [elemental](/Traits.aspx?ID=587) trait. You can also ask questions of, receive answers from, and use the Diplomacy skill with elementals that don’t speak any language.
name: Voice of the Elements

    right="Feat 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Energize Bite](/Feats.aspx?ID=8083) <actions string="Free Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Dragonblood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=794" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Draconic Codex
](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 217

**Prerequisites**
[Breath of the Dragon](/Feats.aspx?ID=5730); [jaws unarmed attack](/Weapons.aspx?ID=356)

**Requirements**
Your most recent action was to use Breath of the Dragon.

</column>

---

The deadly energy of your breath remains in your jaws to empower your attacks. Until the end of your turn, your [jaws](/Weapons.aspx?ID=356) unarmed attack deals an extra 1d6 damage of a type matching that of your [draconic benefactor.](DraconicBenefactors.aspx) This ability has the trait associated with the type of damage it deals.
name: Energize Bite
requirement_markdown: Your most recent action was to use Breath of the Dragon.

    level="1"
    right="Feat 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mineral Deposits](/Feats.aspx?ID=8085) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Dragonblood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=794" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Draconic Codex
](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 217

**Frequency**
once per hour

**Prerequisites**
[Terra Dragonblood](/Feats.aspx?ID=8078)

</column>

---

Precious metals from your bloodstream line your claws, jaws, or the tip of your tail, and you’re able call upon your blood to surge and empower your attacks. Choose [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916) or [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920) and a claw, jaws, or tail unarmed attack that you have. Until the end of your turn, your Strikes with the chosen unarmed attack count as being made of the chosen metal. At 17th level, you can choose [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2915).
name: Mineral Deposits

    right="Feat 13"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Debilitating Breath](/Feats.aspx?ID=8087) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Dragonblood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=794" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Draconic Codex
](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 217

**Prerequisites**
[Breath of the Dragon](/Feats.aspx?ID=5730)

</column>

---

Your breath causes lasting hindrance to those it harms. Creatures who fail their saves against your Breath of the Dragon gain one of the following conditions, based on the damage type of your breath: [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 ([electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), or [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) damage), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1 ([acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), physical, or [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) damage), or [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 ([force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509), or [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) damage). The condition lasts until the end of your next turn.
name: Debilitating Breath

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 17"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ascendant Blood](/Feats.aspx?ID=8089) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Dragonblood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=794" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Draconic Codex
](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 217

**Prerequisites**
[draconic exemplar](DraconicBenefactors.aspx) associated with a specific plane (such as a [diabolic dragon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=398) or [empyreal dragon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=399))

</column>

---

Twice per week, you can cast [_interplanar teleport_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576) as an [innate](/Rules.aspx?ID=2901) spell of the tradition corresponding to your draconic exemplar. This can be used only to travel to the plane associated with your draconic exemplar (for instance, [Heaven](/Planes.aspx?ID=41) if your draconic nature is tied to an empyreal dragon) or from that plane to [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26). Due to your draconic blood, your body serves as the locus, and you don’t require a planar key. If there’s any question about the plane to which your draconic exemplar is associated, the GM determines the answer.
name: Ascendant Blood
prerequisite_markdown: [draconic exemplar](DraconicBenefactors.aspx) associated with a specific plane (such as a [diabolic dragon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=398) or [empyreal dragon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=399))

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Draconic Acolyte Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8090) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Draconic Codex
](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 218

**Archetype** [Draconic Acolyte](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=333)

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)

</column>

---

As an acolyte of dragonkind, you have an item known as a draconic gift that you can use to summon a portion of the magical essence of the dragon you received it from. Choose a draconic benefactor with a trait that matches the trained skill you used to qualify for this feat ([arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1) for [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) for [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43), [occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) for [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44), and [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) for [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)). You can’t change your draconic benefactor later. You gain the [Additional Lore](/Feats.aspx?ID=5114) feat for [Dragon Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41). If you were already trained in Dragon Lore, you also become trained in a Lore skill of your choice. 

You gain a _draconic gift_, a palm-sized magic item of negligible Bulk with a level equal to your level and a trait matching your draconic benefactor’s tradition. Choose this item’s appearance when you take this feat. This item is linked to your spirit, so if you ever lose possession of it (whether by dropping it or having it stolen from your person), it appears back in your possession at the beginning of your next turn.

Finally, you gain the Channel Draconic Essence action and Draconic Salvation reaction. These and all other actions from this archetype gain the tradition trait of your draconic benefactor. 

**Channel Draconic Essence** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Requirements** You aren’t channeling draconic essence and you’re holding or wearing your draconic gift; **Effect** You call down a portion of the magical essence of your draconic benefactor. A Medium spectral dragon that looks like your benefactor appears in any square within 30 feet. This spectral dragon can’t be targeted by any attacks or spells and has no Hit Points, saving throws, or skills. It can occupy the same space as creatures and objects. While your spectral dragon is in your space, you gain a +1 status bonus to saves against [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) and [paralysis](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85). Channel Draconic Essence lasts until you Channel Draconic Essence again, you [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) the effect, the encounter ends, you fall unconscious, or the spectral dragon is farther than 120 feet from you, whichever comes first.

**Draconic Salvation**<actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612)) **Trigger** You critically fail a saving throw against an effect with the same tradition trait as your draconic benefactor (for instance, a divine spell if your benefactor is a diabolic dragon); **Requirements** Your spectral dragon is in your space; **Effect** You improve your saving throw result by one degree of success, and Channel Draconic Essence ends.

<title level="2">Draconic Acolyte Dedication leads to...</title>
[Benefactor's Wings](/Feats.aspx?ID=8095), [Call Draconic Ally](/Feats.aspx?ID=8100), [Deepening Devotion](/Feats.aspx?ID=8097), [Draconic Familiar](/Feats.aspx?ID=8093), [Draconic Fury](/Feats.aspx?ID=8091), [Draconic Resilience](/Feats.aspx?ID=8092), [Essence Overflow](/Feats.aspx?ID=8094), [Frightening Power](/Feats.aspx?ID=8099), [Hidden Hoard](/Feats.aspx?ID=8101), [Reflexive Devotion](/Feats.aspx?ID=8098)
name: Draconic Acolyte Dedication

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Draconic Fury](/Feats.aspx?ID=8091) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Draconic Codex
](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 218

**Archetype** [Draconic Acolyte](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=333)

**Prerequisites**
[Draconic Acolyte Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8090)

**Requirements**
You are holding or wearing your _draconic gift_.

</column>

---

You briefly summon spectral claws that lash out in a flurry. All creatures in a 15-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) take 2d6 slashing damage with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save against the higher of your class DC or spell DC. A creature that critically fails the save also takes 1d4 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). If you are Channeling Draconic Essence, your spectral dragon’s space can be the origin of the cone. 

At 8th level and every 4 levels thereafter, the initial damage increases by 1d6 and the persistent bleed damage on a critical failure increases by 1d4.
name: Draconic Fury
requirement_markdown: You are holding or wearing your _draconic gift_.

>

</title>

Energy reminiscent of dragon scales shimmers over you, making it more difficult for attacks to penetrate your defenses. Until the start of your next turn, you gain a +1 status bonus to AC and gain resistance equal to half your level to bludgeoning damage. If you are Channeling Draconic Essence, you can grant these benefits to a willing creature who is in the same space as your spectral dragon instead of you.

<title level="2">Draconic Resilience leads to...</title>
[Reactive Resilience](/Feats.aspx?ID=8096)

You can select four [familiar](/Rules.aspx?ID=2125) or [master abilities](/Rules.aspx?ID=2126) each day, instead of two, but one of them must always be the dragon familiar ability. >
[Essence Overflow](/Feats.aspx?ID=8094) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />

</column>

---

Uncontainable power explodes from your spectral dragon. Your Channel Draconic Essence ends, and all creatures (except you) in a 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) from your spectral dragon take 6d6 damage with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save against the higher of your class DC or spell DC. The damage’s type matches the breath of your [draconic benefactor](DraconicBenefactors.aspx). You then can’t use Essence Overflow again for 1d4 rounds.

At 8th level and every 2 levels thereafter, the damage increases by 1d6.
name: Essence Overflow
requirement_markdown: You are Channeling Draconic Essence.

Calling on your draconic gift, spectral wings carry you to your destination. You [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312). If you don’t normally have a [fly Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2350), you gain a fly Speed of 20 feet for this movement. If your spectral dragon shares your space, this fly Speed is instead 30 feet, and your spectral dragon moves with you. If you aren’t on solid ground at the end of this movement, you fall.
name: Benefactor's Wings

If you are Channeling Draconic Essence, your spectral dragon can be the origin of this emanation. **Failure** The creature is frightened 2.
**Critical Failure** The creature is frightened 4.

Once per day while you are Channeling Draconic Essence, you can cast [_summon dragon_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1697) as an [innate spell](/Rules.aspx?ID=2901) with a tradition matching your draconic exemplar, heightened to half your level rounded up.

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Prerequisites**
[Draconic Acolyte Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8090)

</column>

---

Your _draconic gift_ is the key to a repository of treasure that only you can access. You create an extradimensional space that can hold up to 100 Bulk worth of objects. The space functions as a [_spacious pouch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3032), but has no Bulk, and can be [Interacted](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) with using only one hand by touching your _draconic gift_. You can normally store and retrieve items that could fit through the opening of a bag (as _spacious pouch_). However, you can store an unattended item larger than would normally fit (but no larger than 10 Bulk) by spending 1 minute concentrating on your _draconic gift_ as you press it against the object. Retrieving it takes 1 minute and causes the item to appear in the closest unoccupied space.
name: Hidden Hoard

</title>

<traits>

<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43); Charisma +1

</column>

---

You gain the service of a young [riding drake](/Companions.aspx?ID=77), [riding dragonet](/Companions.aspx?ID=122), or another animal companion with the [dragon](/Traits.aspx?ID=581) trait you have access to. Your bond with your dragon companion lets you communicate and issue commands to it telepathically with a range of 100 feet. You must still use the [Command an Animal](/Actions.aspx?ID=2400) action to direct it but don’t require words or gestures to do so.

<title level="2">Drake Rider Dedication leads to...</title>
[Flair Rider Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=8105), [Mature Dragon Companion](/Feats.aspx?ID=8103), [War Rider Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=8106), [Winged Leap](/Feats.aspx?ID=8104)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mature Dragon Companion](/Feats.aspx?ID=8103) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 220

**Prerequisites**
[Drake Rider Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8102)

</column>

---

Your dragon companion matures and the bond you share is strengthened. The dragon companion you gained with your Drake Rider Dedication is now a [mature animal companion](/Rules.aspx?ID=2117).

Your dragon companion gains greater independence. During an encounter, even if you don’t use the [Command an Animal](/Actions.aspx?ID=2400) action, your dragon companion can still use 1 action that round on your turn to [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) or [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306). It can do this at any point during your turn, as long as you aren’t currently taking an action. If it does, that’s all the actions it gets that round—you can’t Command it later.

<title level="2">Mature Dragon Companion leads to...</title>
[Incredible Dragon Companion](/Feats.aspx?ID=8107)
name: Mature Dragon Companion

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Winged Leap](/Feats.aspx?ID=8104) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

[dragon companion with the mount special ability](/Rules.aspx?ID=3417); [Drake Rider Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8102)

</column>

---

While drakes and dragons are able flyers, they’re uncomfortable carrying a rider in the air without special training. Through hard work, nerves of steel, and the deep bond you share with your draconic companion, your companion has learned to carry you skyward, if only briefly. Your dragon companion gains the Winged Leap action. 

**Winged Leap** <actions string="Single Action" /> **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** Your dragon companion [Flies](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312). If it doesn’t normally have a [fly Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2350), it gains a fly Speed of 25 feet for this movement. If it isn’t on solid ground at the end of this movement, it falls.

<title level="2">Winged Leap leads to...</title>
[Wing Rider](/Feats.aspx?ID=8108)
name: Winged Leap
prerequisite_markdown: [dragon companion with the mount special ability](/Rules.aspx?ID=3417); [Drake Rider Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8102)

You guide your mount into a plummet, diving headlong at a single foe. You command your mount to [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) twice. It must end this movement at a lower altitude than it began its turn at. You and your mount gain a +4 circumstance bonus to AC against reactions triggered by this movement. At the end of this movement, your mount attempts a melee [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) against one creature in its reach. If the Strike hits, the target is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88) if it’s smaller than your mount; if it’s a critical hit, the target is knocked prone if it’s the same size as your mount or smaller.

    pfs="Standard"

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Draconic Codex
](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 221

**Prerequisites**
[War Rider Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=8106)

**Requirements**
War Rider Stance is active.

</column>

---

You urge your dragon mount forward, wreathed in magical power. Your mount [Strides](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) three times. If your mount has a [fly Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2350), it can substitute a [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) action for any of these Stride actions. As it moves, your dragon companion’s magical breath billows around you. The aura from War Rider Stance increases to a 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) during this movement, and each creature that’s in the emanation at any point during your movement takes the damage from it (with the normal save). A creature can take damage only once, even if you move past it multiple times.
name: Strafing Breath
requirement_markdown: War Rider Stance is active.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Oatia Skysage Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8112) <actions string="" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Gatewalkers</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 238

**Archetype** [Oatia Skysage](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=335)

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)

</column>

---

Your study of the stars unlocks supernatural insights and magic. You either become trained in [Astronomy Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) or an expert in [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44).

You learn to cast [spontaneous spells](/Rules.aspx?ID=2224) and gain the [Cast a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) activity. You gain a spell repertoire with two of the following cantrips of your choice: [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [_guidance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1549), [_know the way_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1582), or [_read aura_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646). You cast these spells as occult spells as you draw on the stars and night sky. You’re trained in occult spell attack rolls and spell DCs. Your key spellcasting ability for these spells is Intelligence.

<title level="2">Oatia Skysage Dedication leads to...</title>
[Basic Skysage Divination](/Feats.aspx?ID=8113), [Minor Omen](/Feats.aspx?ID=8116), [Night's Glow](/Feats.aspx?ID=8115), [Night's Shine](/Feats.aspx?ID=8120), [Night's Warning](/Feats.aspx?ID=8121), [Scholar's Hunch](/Feats.aspx?ID=8117), [Stargazer's Eyes](/Feats.aspx?ID=8114), [Starlight Armor](/Feats.aspx?ID=8122), [Starlit Spells](/Feats.aspx?ID=8118)
name: Oatia Skysage Dedication

At 6th level, choose one of the following 2nd-rank spells to add to your spell repertoire: [_augury_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1445) or [_see the unseen_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663). At 8th level, you choose one of the following 3rd-rank spells to add to your spell repertoire: [_clairaudience_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1465) or [_locate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1588).

This feat grants a focus pool of 1 Focus Point that you can recover by using the [Refocus](/Rules.aspx?ID=1442) to reflect upon or gaze at the stars.

**Special** You can take this feat a second time, gaining the focus spell that you didn’t gain the first time. Increase the number of Focus Points in your pool by 1.

A creature targets you with an attack and you can see the attacker.

**Requirements**

</column>

---

Portents written in the night sky show you how to avoid an attack. The triggering attack roll targets your Occultism DC instead of your AC. Though this allows you to avoid taking penalties to your AC, it doesn’t remove any conditions or other effects causing such penalties. For example, an enemy with sneak attack would still deal extra damage to you for being [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58), even though you wouldn’t take the –2 circumstance penalty when defending against the attack.
name: Night's Warning

    right="Feat 14"
    pfs="Limited"

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=120" />

once per day

**Prerequisites**
[Oatia Skysage Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8112)

</column>

---

You armor yourself in the light of distant suns, shielding yourself from harm. The armor grants you [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 7 to piercing, bludgeoning, and slashing damage. The armor also sheds [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2402) for 20 feet (and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2403) for the next 20 feet).

Whenever an adjacent creature attacks you, the attacker must attempt a Will save against your spell DC at the end of its action. On a failure, it becomes [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) until the end of its next turn. Regardless of the result of the save, the attacker is temporarily immune until the end of its next turn. The dazzling effect has the [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640) and [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727) traits.

When you reach 17th level, the resistance increases to 10.
name: Starlight Armor

You choose one of the following 7th-rank spells to add to your spell repertoire: [_retrocognition_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1652) or [_true target_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1726). At 18th level, add [_pinpoint_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1633) to your spell repertoire, and at 20th level, add [_foresight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1537) to your spell repertoire. You gain master proficiency with spell attack rolls and spell DCs for occult spells.
name: Master Skysage Divination

[Familiar Master Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=6328); you have befriended a spirit guide and it bonded with you using its Bond to Mortal ability.

</column>

---

You gain a spirit guide ally. Your familiar takes on the form of a [spirit guide](/Familiars.aspx?Specific=true&ID=23) specific familiar.

**Special** You can take Scion of Domora Dedication even if you have the Familiar Master Dedication and have not taken two other Familiar Master feats. You can’t select another dedication feat until you have gained at least two other feats from the scion of Domora or familiar master archetypes.

<title level="2">Scion of Domora Dedication leads to...</title>
[Guardian's Embrace](/Feats.aspx?ID=8127), [Guided Skill](/Feats.aspx?ID=8126), [Invigorating Breath](/Feats.aspx?ID=8128), [Spirit Guide Form](/Feats.aspx?ID=8131), [Spiritual Aid](/Feats.aspx?ID=8130), [Spiritual Flurry](/Feats.aspx?ID=8129), [Spiritual Strike](/Feats.aspx?ID=8125)

</title>

</column>

---

Your spirit guide enhances your attacks with occult power. Make a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) with a weapon that has at least one fundamental rune. This counts as two attacks when calculating your multiple attack penalty. The Strike has the effects of the [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840) property rune and deals an additional 2d6 force damage. If your weapon has a [_major striking_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) rune, this Strike instead deals 3d6 force damage.

<title level="2">Spiritual Strike leads to...</title>
[Spiritual Flurry](/Feats.aspx?ID=8129)

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

You are adjacent to your spirit guide or it is sharing your space.

</column>

---

Your spirit guide discorporates to surround you in an invisible protective shroud. You gain a +2 status bonus to AC against physical attacks until the beginning of your next turn. If you are hit by a physical attack during this time, you can use a reaction to gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) 10 to physical damage dealt by that attack; doing so ends the AC bonus, and you can’t use Guardian’s Embrace again for 10 minutes. Your spirit guide cannot be targeted by effects, cannot be affected by area effects, and cannot take any actions while this ability is active. Your spirit guide appears in a space adjacent to you when the effect ends.
name: Guardian's Embrace

**Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 241 **Archetype** [Scions of Domora](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=336) **Frequency** once per day **Prerequisites** [Scion of Domora Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8124) --- You fuse with your spirit guide, becoming a hybrid being with enhanced physical and spiritual power. This effect lasts 1 minute; you can end it early as a free action. During this time, you can’t use any other actions granted by this archetype. You gain the following statistics and abilities, regardless of your type of spirit guide.
  • You become [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=632).
  • You gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) 10 to all damage (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840), or [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737); double resistance vs. non-magical).
  • Darkvision.
  • An unarmed melee attack of the same type as your spirit guide (either claws or jaws), which is the only attack you can Strike with. You’re trained with this attack. Your attack modifier is +29 or your normal unarmed attack modifier, whichever is higher. You use the listed statistics for the unarmed attack type:
  • **Claws** ([agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [backstabber](/Traits.aspx?ID=544), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)) , **Damage** 3d6+5 slashing plus 1d6 force;
  • **Jaws** ([forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=611)) Damage 3d10+5 piercing plus 1d6 force.
name: Spirit Guide Form

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Gatewalkers</spoilers>

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47); you are an Ilverani elf

</column>

---

You have carefully studied the histories, customs, and etiquette of many of the younger peoples of Golarion. You become an expert in [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47). At 7th level, you become a master in Society, and at 15th level, you become legendary in Society. You can use the Society skill instead of the [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) skill when you attempt to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392) on a non-elven intelligent humanoid creature.

<title level="2">Twilight Speaker Dedication leads to...</title>
[Betraying Shank](/Feats.aspx?ID=8135), [Empathetic Envoy](/Feats.aspx?ID=8133), [Ilverani Purist](/Feats.aspx?ID=8136), [World-Wise Vigilance](/Feats.aspx?ID=8137)
name: Twilight Speaker Dedication
prerequisite_markdown: Trained in [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47); you are an Ilverani elf

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Empathetic Envoy](/Feats.aspx?ID=8133) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 242

**Archetype** [Twilight Speaker](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=337)

**Prerequisites**
[Twilight Speaker Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8132)

</column>

---

You believe that treating others with respect is the fastest way into their hearts, and in turn others are more likely to believe in your good intentions and write off bad first impressions as flukes. If a creature’s attitude toward you becomes lower over the course of a social interaction (for example, from [friendly](/Conditions.aspx?ID=75) to [indifferent](/Conditions.aspx?ID=82), or from indifferent to [unfriendly](/Conditions.aspx?ID=97)), their impression of you returns to its starting level an hour after the social interaction ends. This ability has no effect if the creature you are interacting with becomes [hostile](/Conditions.aspx?ID=80).
name: Empathetic Envoy

markdown:
**Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 242 **Archetype** [Twilight Speaker](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=337) **Trigger** You are the target of a melee attack and the attacker has not rolled yet. **Requirements** You are aware of the attacker, the attacker is an intelligent humanoid creature, and you have not attempted to harm the attacker. --- With a wide and sincere smile, you give your attacker pause. You attempt a [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) check against the triggering attacker’s Will DC. After you use Disarming Smile, all creatures who witnessed you use it are temporarily immune to your Disarming Smile for the next 24 hours. **Critical Success** Your enemy ceases their attack, momentarily taken aback by your disarming smile. The attack fails and the triggering target can’t attempt [hostile actions](/Rules.aspx?ID=2251) against you until the beginning of its next turn or until you (or your allies) take hostile actions against the enemy (or its allies). You can begin talking to the creature on your next turn to attempt another [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) check; on a success, you [sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) the effect until the beginning of your next turn, to a maximum of 1 minute. Talking on subsequent rounds requires that you be able to communicate with the target creature and imparts the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) and [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642) traits to the action.
**Success** Your foe pauses momentarily. Their attack fails, but they can attempt further attacks against you.
**Failure** The target’s attack is unaffected. Disarming Smile leads to... [Emphatic Emissary](/Feats.aspx?ID=8138)
name: Disarming Smile
requirement_markdown: You are aware of the attacker, the attacker is an intelligent humanoid creature, and you have not attempted to harm the attacker.

---

While your usual diplomacy is well intentioned, you know that sometimes those same skills will need to be turned to deception. In a flash, you draw a sheathed or concealed [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526) or [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602) weapon and make a melee Strike. The target is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) against your Strike. You then roll [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) for initiative.

markdown:
May contain spoilers from Revenge of the Runelords **Source** [Pathfinder Adventure Path #219: Lord of the Trinity Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=283) pg. 72 **Archetype** [Avenging Runelord](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=338) --- The call of a long dead runelord has infected your soul and transformed your fate. Choose one of the following sins: envy, gluttony, greed, sloth, or wrath. You become trained in [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35). If you are already trained in Arcana, you become trained in the skill of your choice. At 14th level, you gain expert proficiency in Arcana. At 16th level, if you gain master proficiency in Arcana.
You have familiarity with common weapons in the [polearm](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=11) and [spear](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=14) weapon groups—for the purposes of proficiency, you treat any of these that are martial weapons as common weapons. When your proficiency for simple weapons and any that are advanced weapons as martial weapons. You learn the Thassilonian language.
You gain the ability to cast an arcane spell as an innate spell. This spell must be chosen from the following list: [_darkvision_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1481), [_mystic armor_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1611), [_runic body_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1657), [_runic weapon_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1658), [_see the unseen_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663), [_sending_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1665), or [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727); alternatively, you can choose any spell of 6th rank or lower from the sin spells listed for your chosen sin (see [School of Thassilonian Rune Magic](/ArcaneSchools.aspx?ID=25)). You can cast this spell as an arcane innate spell once per day. The spell is automatically heightened to a spell rank equal to half your level rounded up. Use mythic proficiency to determine any attack rolls or spell DCs. Your obsession with revenge allows you to reflexively lash out at those who harm you with blasts of arcane magic. You gain the Runelord's Response reaction. Avenging Runelord Dedication leads to... [Apocalyptic Visions](/Feats.aspx?ID=8149), [Autonomous Arms](/Feats.aspx?ID=8145), [Craft Runewell](/Feats.aspx?ID=8150), [Expanded Runelord Magic](/Feats.aspx?ID=8140), [Reliable Magic](/Feats.aspx?ID=8141), [Shining Runes](/Feats.aspx?ID=8142), [Subjugation](/Feats.aspx?ID=8146), [Vindictive Thoughts](/Feats.aspx?ID=8147), [Willing Runes](/Feats.aspx?ID=8143), [Writhing Runelord Weapon](/Feats.aspx?ID=8144)
name: Avenging Runelord Dedication

Your ability to use magic associated with your chosen sin grows. You gain the ability to cast one more arcane spell as an innate spell. This spell must be chosen from those listed in Avenging Runelord Dedication above, as well as [_contingency_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1472), [_spell riposte_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2027), or any 7th-rank spell from your chosen sin’s school. You can cast this spell as an arcane innate spell once per day. The spell is automatically heightened to a spell rank equal to half your level rounded up. Use mythic proficiency to determine any attack rolls or spell DCs.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 14"
    pfs=""
>
[Reliable Magic](/Feats.aspx?ID=8141) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder Adventure Path #219: Lord of the Trinity Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=283) pg. 73

**Archetype** [Avenging Runelord](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=338)

**Prerequisites**
[Avenging Runelord Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8139)

</column>

---

Magic stored in written form or within wands is more reliable in your capable hands. Whenever you overcharge a wand, reduce the DC of the flat check to avoid destroying the wand by 4. It still gains the broken condition on a success.

When you [Cast a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) from a scroll you can spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3323) as a free action. The spell is cast at mythic proficiency. Once you cast the spell using this ability, attempt a DC 11 flat check. If this check is successful, the scroll is not destroyed, and instead glows with light equivalent to that of a candle for 10 minutes. You can Cast a Spell from this scroll again, but if you do so you cannot enhance it in any way (such as by using this feat), and the scroll is destroyed. If not used before the end of the 10 minutes, the scroll is destroyed.
name: Reliable Magic

They may blaze brightly, leave lingering fog, create discordant noise, or fill the air with an overwhelming scent, such that their presence obfuscates you from onlookers. You gain concealment from enemy creatures until the beginning of your next turn. The DC to [identify](/Rules.aspx?ID=2249) your spells as you cast them is 2 higher than normal.

**Source** [Pathfinder Adventure Path #219: Lord of the Trinity Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=283) pg. 73 **Archetype** [Avenging Runelord](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=338) **Prerequisites** [Avenging Runelord Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8139) --- Magical markings of every kind seem eager to aid your endeavors. Whenever you read text, be it mundane or magical, the writing itself seems to almost to leap into your thoughts. You gain a +2 status bonus to checks to [Decipher Writing](/Actions.aspx?ID=2363), [Identify Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=2365), [Learn a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2366), or any [Research](/Rules.aspx?ID=3045) check or [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) check involving reading.
You gain a +2 status bonus to saving throws and to your AC against effects from hazards that involve reading, such as those created by a [_rune trap_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=124) ritual. If you were the one who triggered the hazard by reading the triggering runes, the status bonus increases to +4.
If you prepare spells from a spellbook, you gain the following activity.
name: Willing Runes

Your prowess with long-hafted weapons catches your opponents by surprise, as it bends or elongates in an obviously supernatural manner. Spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3323). Your weapon gains the [reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=684) trait until the end of your turn, if it doesn’t have it already. You can Stride and Strike twice, in any order. Apply your [multiple attack penalty](/Rules.aspx?ID=2289) to your Strikes as normal, but the target of your second Strike is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) to it.

</title>

</column>

---

A portion of the ancient grasping spirit that infuses your being spills over into a spear or polearm you carry. When you perform your daily preparations, choose one spear or polearm you own. This weapon becomes infused with arcane magic and fragments of thought—it doesn’t become fully intelligent, but it instinctually recognizes you as its wielder and denies its abilities to any other wielder while you live. If your weapon doesn’t possess it already, it gains the effects of the [animated](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2831) property rune. This does not count against the maximum number of property runes your weapon can have at one time. You also gain the following action. 

<document level="2" id="action-3528" />

markdown:
**Source** [Pathfinder Adventure Path #219: Lord of the Trinity Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=283) pg. 74 **Archetype** [Avenging Runelord](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=338) **Prerequisites** [Avenging Runelord Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8139) --- You have managed to gain a degree of control over the runelord’s ancient soul within your mind, enough that you can project this invasive influence into the minds of others. Spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3323). You attempt to send your runelord’s influence into the mind of a creature you can sense within 30 feet. The creature attempts a Will save against your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher. Giants take a –2 status penalty to this saving throw. Regardless of the result, the target is temporarily immune to this ability for 24 hours once the Subjugation ends. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 as it fights off your runelord’s influence. You gain awareness of its abilities and weaknesses as if you achieved a critical success on a [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) check to identify the creature.
**Failure** The creature relinquishes control of its faculties and abilities. You gain awareness of its abilities and weaknesses as success. You [control](/Conditions.aspx?ID=64) the creature until the end of its next turn.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but you control the creature for 1 minute.
name: Subjugation

At 18th level, the mental damage increases to 11d6, and at 20th level, the mental damage increases to 12d6.

markdown:
**Source** [Pathfinder Adventure Path #219: Lord of the Trinity Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=283) pg. 75 **Archetype** [Avenging Runelord](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=338) **Frequency** Once per day **Prerequisites** [Avenging Runelord Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8139) --- Your runelord may not have lived long enough to need to retreat into hiding in the face of Earthfall, but the apocalyptic echoes of this event nevertheless reached their soul all the way in the Boneyard. You’ve learned to endure the invasive thoughts of this empire-ending event, but now you can project that despair into the minds of others. Spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3323) and target up to 10 creatures within 60 feet that you can sense. You force images into their minds of Earthfall, forcing them to endure a vision of the end of the world and all they hold dear. The creatures must attempt a Will save against your class DC or spell DC using mythic proficiency, whichever is higher. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is so distracted by the visions that they are [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is slowed 1 and [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** The creature is slowed 2 and stupefied 2 for 1 minute. In addition, the creature suffers the effects of the spell [_nightmare_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1615) the next time it rests. If the creature fails or critically fails against this effect, the effect repeats each time the creature attempts to sleep until it succeeds on its save or 6 days have passed. You do not need to know the creature’s name or remain on the same planet for the creature to experience these nightmares.
name: Apocalyptic Visions

<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<additional-info>
**Source** [Pathfinder Adventure Path #219: Lord of the Trinity Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=283) pg. 75

**Frequency**
Once per day

**Prerequisites**
[Avenging Runelord Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8139)

</additional-info>

---

<summary>
Your runelord may not have lived long enough to need to retreat into hiding in the face of Earthfall, but the apocalyptic echoes of this event nevertheless reached their soul all the way in the Boneyard.
</summary>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 20"
    pfs=""
>
[Craft Runewell](/Feats.aspx?ID=8150) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder Adventure Path #219: Lord of the Trinity Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=283) pg. 75

**Archetype** [Avenging Runelord](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=338)

**Prerequisites**
[Avenging Runelord Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8139)

</column>

---

Even if the runelord whose soul influences you never did so themselves, you know how to create your own _runewell_—an artifact that grants you a measure of immortality. You learn the [create demiplane](/Rituals.aspx?ID=191) ritual. To create your _runewell_, you must cast _create demiplane_ heightened to 10th-rank. When you do so, you also create an _anima focus_ (an immobile object like a statue or fortification with an appearance you design) at the location you cast the ritual, connecting your other-worldly home to this location. Once you create your _runewell_, you cannot create another unless the current one is destroyed.

While you are located on the same plane that your _anima focus_ is located on, or while you are within your _runewell_, any [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) value you gain stops at doomed 3. The only way an adversary can destroy your _anima focus_ or _runewell_ is to reduce you to 0 Hit Points and doomed 3 and then reduce your _anima focus_ to 0 Hit Points using arcane magic (or an agent affected by arcane magic) from one of the schools in opposition to your specialized sin chosen when you took Avenging Runelord Dedication. Your _anima focus_ has Hardness equal to twice your level and Hit Points equal to your own. Your _anima focus_ has [fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=62) equal to twice your Intelligence modifier. 

Your _anima focus_ is associated with the same sin as your chosen avenging runelord sin. As long as at least one sizable settlement in which this sin is prevalent (subject to the GM’s discretion) is within a hundred miles of it, your _anima focus_ absorbs energy from these sins. As long as it does so and you are on the same plane as the _anima focus_, you do not age and are immune to effects that rob you of your mental abilities (such as the [_never mind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1614) spell or the [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) and [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) conditions).

Other creatures cannot enter your _runewell_ unless they possess a _planar key_ you created and gave to them, or unless they’re adjacent to your _anima focus_. Your _runewell_ has an exit that allows adjacent creatures within to activate with an Interact action; when they do so, they exit the runewell and appear adjacent to your _anima focus_. You gain the following activity.

**Enter Runewell** 10 minutes ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) You and up to 6 willing creatures you’re touching are instantly transported into your _runewell_, regardless of any distance between you and your _anima focus_.
name: Craft Runewell

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 12"
    pfs=""
>
[Heroic Scion Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8151) <actions string="" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Revenge of the Runelords</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Destiny" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=838" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder Adventure Path #219: Lord of the Trinity Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=283) pg. 77

**Archetype** [Heroic Scion](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=339)

</column>

---

Destiny calls, and the soul of a dead hero reincarnates to take up the fight! Either an old enemy resumes their efforts to affect chaos and violence, or a new threat arises and you feel compelled to stand against it. You’ve stood against evil before, and the time is nigh for you to do so once again.

When you select this mythic destiny, work with your GM to determine the best choice for which previous PC or NPC serves as the source of your reincarnation. For the Revenge of the Runelords Adventure Path in particular, any PC who played a role in the Rise of the Runelords, Shattered Star, or Return of the Runelords Adventure Paths makes for a perfect fit—as would any allied NPC who, in your previous campaign, perished before the end.

A heroic scion comes into the world when a nemesis from their old adventures returns. In the case of this campaign, the heroic scion’s nemesis are the ancient runelords of Thassilon. In other campaigns, this nemesis can be a specific individual, a coterie of rivals, or an organization you’re destined to oppose. You instinctively know when your nemesis is active in the world. This knowledge may begin as nightmares or a feeling of inevitable conflict but grows stronger and more specific as you contemplate further. You also know when this entity or group has been fully defeated and ceases to operate at all. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) or [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302) whenever an ally or servant of your nemesis attempts to Lie to you, escape your notice, or Impersonate someone else.

Fate has deemed you incredibly hard to kill. You reduce the DC of [recovery checks](/Rules.aspx?ID=2326) by 1.

Choose one skill that’s not a Lore skill, based on the themes of your reincarnated heroic source. Your GM can help you determine a list of appropriate skills to choose from. When you employ an action using that skill, you may spend a Mythic Point to attempt the associated check at mythic proficiency.

Finally, you gain the [Additional Lore](/Feats.aspx?ID=5114) feat for a special Lore skill subcategory—Incarnation Lore. This Lore skill allows you to become aware of or recall your past exploits. When you increase your proficiency in Incarnation Lore, you remember more about your previous adventures. Certain heroic scion feats require you to attempt an Incarnation Lore check, such as when you attempt the following activity granted you by this feat. 

<document level="2" id="action-3529" />

<title level="2">Heroic Scion Dedication leads to...</title>
[Avoid Fate's Gaze](/Feats.aspx?ID=8152), [Daunting Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=8158), [Eternal Hero](/Feats.aspx?ID=8165), [I've Been Here Before](/Feats.aspx?ID=8153), [Internal Dialogue](/Feats.aspx?ID=8154), [Let Death Be My Weapon](/Feats.aspx?ID=8159), [Momentary Respite](/Feats.aspx?ID=8160), [My Reputation Precedes Me](/Feats.aspx?ID=8155), [On My Best Day](/Feats.aspx?ID=8162), [Reincarnated Companion](/Feats.aspx?ID=8156), [Telling Blow](/Feats.aspx?ID=8163), [To Me!](/Feats.aspx?ID=8164), [To Thine Own Self](/Feats.aspx?ID=8157), [Vigorous Victor](/Feats.aspx?ID=8161)
name: Heroic Scion Dedication

[Avoid Fate's Gaze](/Feats.aspx?ID=8152) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Prerequisites**
[Heroic Scion Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8151)

</column>

---

Your past and current enemies may know you by reputation, but spying on you with magic is much more difficult. Whenever you attempt a saving throw against a scrying or effect, you do so at mythic proficiency. Scrying effects that attempt to observe you that don’t allow saving throws to resist (such as [_clairaudience_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1465) or [_clairvoyance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1466)) treat you as if you were concealed and actively trying to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) or [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405) unless they are mythic as well.
name: Avoid Fate's Gaze

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 14"
    pfs=""
>
[I've Been Here Before](/Feats.aspx?ID=8153) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Exploration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=66" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder Adventure Path #219: Lord of the Trinity Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=283) pg. 77

**Archetype** [Heroic Scion](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=339)

**Prerequisites**
[Heroic Scion Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8151)

</column>

---

You adapt quickly to new settings and terrains. When you make your daily preparations in a region you’ve never visited before (such as a city, geographical region, or small demiplane, but not in a dungeon, structure, or other location that focuses on encounter mode play), you spend part of that time introspectively digging into the memories of your past life to get a feel for the adventure that awaits. Choose one of the following Exploration activities: [Avoid Notice](/Actions.aspx?ID=2622), [Defend](/Actions.aspx?ID=2623), [Investigate](/Actions.aspx?ID=2627), [Scout](/Actions.aspx?ID=2629), or [Search](/Actions.aspx?ID=2630). You perform that exploration activity at full speed rather than at half speed, and any skill check you attempt as a result of this Exploration activity is made at mythic proficiency. These benefits end as soon as you enter a new region, as determined by the GM.
name: I've Been Here Before

At 18th level, these circumstance bonuses increase to +3. If you spend a [Mythic point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3323) when you attempt such a check, you do so at mythic proficiency.

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

[Heroic Scion Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8151); [familiar](/Rules.aspx?ID=2121) or [nimble](/Companions.aspx?Type=Advancement&amp;ID=7) or [savage](/Companions.aspx?Type=Advancement&amp;ID=8) animal companion

</column>

---

You’re not alone in having a legendary past life—so too does your companion. This previous life might be the return of an animal companion or familiar a previous PC of yours had that perished, or it could be the reincarnation of an entirely different legendary creature. It could even be the returning spirit of a close friend or ally who stood by your side. If you have an animal companion, it gains one specialization of your choice. If you have a familiar, it gains two additional familiar abilities. If your fated companion is slain, you can replace it at no cost when you perform your daily preparations.
name: Reincarnated Companion

**Source** [Pathfinder Adventure Path #219: Lord of the Trinity Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=283) pg. 78 **Archetype** [Heroic Scion](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=339) **Prerequisites** [Heroic Scion Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8151) --- Your enemies feel the inevitability of defeat once they feel the potency of your magic. If your next action is to [Cast a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) that allows a saving throw, your spell gains the [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), and [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) traits. The spell gains the following success and failure effects in addition to any other effects it would normally have. If the target is your nemesis or an ally of your nemesis, use mythic proficiency to determine your spell’s attack bonus and save DC; if the spell has multiple targets, all targets must be your nemesis or allied with your nemesis for you to use mythic proficiency. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected by the daunting spell.
**Success** The target is [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1.
**Failure** The target is frightened 2 and [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for as long as they remain frightened. If the daunting spell has a duration, the affected creature can’t reduce their frightened condition from this effect below 1 until the spell’s duration ends.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but frightened 3 and stupefied 2.
name: Daunting Spell

    right="Feat 16"
    pfs=""
>
[Let Death Be My Weapon](/Feats.aspx?ID=8159) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Archetype** [Heroic Scion](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=339)

**Prerequisites**
[Heroic Scion Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8151)

</column>

---

You recall your death in vivid detail, and the fear, pain, and uncertainty you suffered are blunted by the truth of your reincarnation. Pain and agony brought on by your foes can be endured and even turned back upon them when you feel yourself at death’s door. Whenever you have the [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69), or [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=99) condition, you gain that condition’s value as a status bonus to damage rolls when you make melee or ranged weapon Strikes. If you have multiple conditions, only the highest value condition counts for setting the amount of this damage bonus. You also gain the following free action. 

<document level="2" id="action-3530" />
name: Let Death Be My Weapon

<traits>
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

**Source** [Pathfinder Adventure Path #219: Lord of the Trinity Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=283) pg. 78

You regain 8d8 Hit Points and remove any [persistent damage](/Rules.aspx?ID=2306) condition affecting you. Until the end of your next turn, you’re immune to persistent damage (though you would take any other effects of an attack or spell that would also give you persistent damage).

Long years of success have not only shown you where your limits lie, they’ve expanded them. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to saving throws against effects that would cause you to become [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91), or [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92). Whenever you attempt such a save, you can spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3323) do so at mythic proficiency. The bonus increases to +3 at 18th level.

**Source** [Pathfinder Adventure Path #219: Lord of the Trinity Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=283) pg. 79 **Archetype** [Heroic Scion](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=339) **Frequency** once per hour **Prerequisites** [Heroic Scion Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8151) --- Be they in your current life or your previous one, you’ve had many heroic moments that continue to inspire you. Even past failures or humiliations can become a source of inspiration—or at least, an opportunity to make up for a past error on this new day. Attempt an Incarnation Lore check against a very hard DC of your level. **Critical Success** You gain a +3 status bonus to AC and [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) to physical damage equal to your level until the end of your next turn. You can [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) this effect for up to 1 minute.
**Success** As critical success, but the status bonus is +2 and the resistance is equal to half your level.
**Failure** You gain a +1 status bonus to AC until the end of your next turn.
**Critical Failure** You gain a +1 status bonus to AC against the next attack made against you until the end of your next turn.
name: On My Best Day

[Telling Blow](/Feats.aspx?ID=8163) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Flourish" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=73" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

</column>

---

Your determination empowers a telling blow to defeat your most powerful enemies. Spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3323). Make a Strike at mythic proficiency. If the Strike hits, you deal an extra die of weapon damage. You can spend additional Mythic Points after determining the outcome of the attack to deal one more die of weapon damage per extra Mythic Point spent. If the target of your attack is your nemesis or an ally of your nemesis, you gain two die of weapon damage per extra Mythic Point spent.

If you have no Mythic Points remaining after using this ability, you gain the [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73) condition.
name: Telling Blow

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 18"
    pfs=""
>
[To Me!](/Feats.aspx?ID=8164) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Auditory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=16" />
<trait label="Emotion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=60" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
<trait label="Visual" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=163" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder Adventure Path #219: Lord of the Trinity Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=283) pg. 79

**Archetype** [Heroic Scion](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=339)

**Frequency**
once per day

**Prerequisites**
[Heroic Scion Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8151)

</column>

---

Two lives spent in adventuring groups has given you a special gift for bolstering party morale and pushing your allies to achieve greater ends when they work as a team. Spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3323) to rally your allies to your side to regroup or push your advantage. Stride and then make a melee Strike. As soon as your turn is over, your allies within 30 feet who can see and hear you may Stride in your direction as a free action, but don’t have to. Your allies control their own movement and may move in such a way as to avoid obstacles or creatures, but if they Stride, they must be at least 5 feet closer to you than where they were when they started by the end of this movement. If yours or an ally’s movement this round triggers reactions, you or your ally uses mythic proficiency to determine their AC or to attempt a saving throw to resist the reaction. If an ally is within melee range of the target of the Strike you made with this ability, that ally may also make a melee Strike against that enemy as a reaction instead of Striding toward you.

Any Strikes from To Me! made against your nemesis, be they Strikes you make or Strikes made by allies, use mythic proficiency.
name: To Me!

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 20"
    pfs=""
>
[Eternal Hero](/Feats.aspx?ID=8165) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder Adventure Path #219: Lord of the Trinity Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=283) pg. 79

**Archetype** [Heroic Scion](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=339)

**Prerequisites**
[Heroic Scion Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8151)

</column>

---

You might be protected by divine intervention or the necessity of cosmic balance. You might be watched over by agents of destiny or fate. You could even be simply someone whose luck and skill have always worked together to ensure your triumph, no matter the odds you might face. Whatever the explanation, you’re almost impossible to kill. You reduce the DC of [recovery checks](/Rules.aspx?ID=2326) by 1 (this is cumulative with the reduction from the Heroic Scion Dedication feat). Whenever your [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) condition would increase to 4 or more, it doesn’t increase. The first time each day you would be reduced to 0 Hit Points or outright killed by magical means, you instead remain at 1 Hit Point and become immune to all damage until the end of your next turn.

If you finally do die, you can spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3323) as a free action to immediately reincarnate—manifesting in a new body with the same statistics (save for potential changes to your ancestry and ancestry feats) within 15 feet of where your death occurred. Each time you repeat this reincarnation, the cost to do so increases by 1, so that after your third reincarnation, you can no longer swiftly return (as no mythic character can ever have more than 3 Mythic Points at one time).
name: Eternal Hero

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 12"
    pfs=""
>
[Timewracked Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8166) <actions string="" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Revenge of the Runelords</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Destiny" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=838" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder Adventure Path #219: Lord of the Trinity Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=283) pg. 80

**Archetype** [Timewracked](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=340)

</column>

---

The current of time shifts subtly in your field of view. You have learned to tread these temporal eddies, moving forward and pushing backward in small strokes—sometimes even gaining the ability to move your allies and enemies through time as you choose. Whether or not you’ll ever be fully restored to your proper timeline, you can’t predict—but until then you continue to manipulate your estrangement from time to move unseen, defy your opponents in combat, and occupy future or past versions of yourself to escape distress. With practice you could gain the ability to rehearse a speech at the same tie you deliver it or attack distant foes without spending the time to move between them. Eventually, you might gain the power to vanish from existence altogether while you hop forward and backward in time or even become immortal.

Your Speed increases by 5 feet for each mode of movement available to you. You can spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3323) to roll any check made to determine your initiative at mythic proficiency. If your initiative roll result is tied with an opponent’s initiative roll, you can choose to go first. In addition, you gain the following reaction. 

<document level="2" id="action-3531" />

<title level="2">Timewracked Dedication leads to...</title>
[Be Right Back](/Feats.aspx?ID=8167), [Bolstered Recovery](/Feats.aspx?ID=8172), [Fracture Time Flow](/Feats.aspx?ID=8175), [Live Off Borrowed Time](/Feats.aspx?ID=8168), [Not This Time](/Feats.aspx?ID=8176), [Now You See Me](/Feats.aspx?ID=8169), [Preserve the Moment](/Feats.aspx?ID=8174), [Second Sight](/Feats.aspx?ID=8170), [Temporal Fury](/Feats.aspx?ID=8171), [Timelessness](/Feats.aspx?ID=8177)
name: Timewracked Dedication

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />

the Price of the chosen item

**Frequency**

**Prerequisites**
[Timewracked Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8166)

</column>

---

You can manipulate time to produce an item in a seemingly impossible fashion. You flicker a few times as you make adjustments to your gear, then solidify again with a specific needed item that you purchased from an alternate timeline using coin you have on your person. This allows you to purchase a single common item with a level no higher than your level and its Bulk must be low enough that carrying it wouldn’t have made you encumbered. You must spend the standard amount to purchase the item, and this money must be carried on your person. You can be wearing or holding the item, as long as you have the required number of hands free, and if the item has the [invested](/Traits.aspx?ID=637) trait, you have already [Invested](/Rules.aspx?ID=3140) in it, provided you haven’t reached your limit of invested items.
name: Be Right Back

**Source** [Pathfinder Adventure Path #219: Lord of the Trinity Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=283) pg. 82 **Archetype** [Timewracked](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=340) **Prerequisites** [Timewracked Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8166) --- You peer into the next several seconds to watch the potential timelines around a nearby creature unfold. Spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3323), then make a check to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) at mythic proficiency about a creature you can see. This check gains the following additional effects. **Critical Success** You learn all the actions the creature will take on its next turn (or in the first round of combat if combat hasn’t begun), such as “Stride toward Seelah” or “Cast _shield_.” These actions are locked in and the creature must attempt them, even if the situation changes, though it need not perform the actions in the order stated. For instance, if the creature locked in the actions “Stride toward Seelah,” “Make a longsword Strike against Seelah,” and “Raise a shield,” and Seelah spent her turn moving adjacent to the creature, the creature can make the longsword Strike as its first action if it wishes. If the creature is unable to fulfill an action it committed to, it loses that action as it reels from a temporal paradox. If the situation changes drastically (perhaps Seelah becomes invisible and the creature is unaware where Seelah is), the GM determines what happens.
**Success** You gain one additional piece of information about the creature, as if you had critically succeeded at the [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) check.
name: Second Sight

**Archetype** [Timewracked](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=340)

**Prerequisites**
[Timewracked Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8166)

</column>

---

You shift and skip through time, vanishing during your movement to appear in unexpected and advantageous locations for combat. Spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3323). Strike, then Step up to 10 feet. This movement is affected normally by barriers and [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366); you still move physically through the area, just so swiftly that it appears that you’ve teleported. Then, make another Strike. Apply your [multiple attack penalty](/Rules.aspx?ID=2289) to both Strikes normally, but this second Strike is made at mythic proficiency and your target is [off guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) against it.

<title level="2">Temporal Fury leads to...</title>
[Echoing Fury](/Feats.aspx?ID=8173)
name: Temporal Fury

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 16"
    pfs=""
>
[Preserve the Moment](/Feats.aspx?ID=8174) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=120" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder Adventure Path #219: Lord of the Trinity Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=283) pg. 82

**Archetype** [Timewracked](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=340)

**Frequency**
once per day

**Prerequisites**
[Timewracked Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8166)

**Trigger**
Your turn ends.

</column>

---

Sometimes, your actions are so successful and impressive that it’s worth going back to them. Note the actions you took during the turn that just ended (excepting the reaction you used to trigger Preserve the Moment), including the die roll results (not the actual check results). Die rolls you made as a result of others’ actions on you or from the environment are not recorded. For example, if you Stride up to a firewyrm, roll a basic Reflex save against its intense heat aura, then Strike the firewyrm with a longsword, and then Climb up an adjacent wall, when you activate Preserve the Moment you record only the Stride action, the Strike action (and the die roll result you roll for the Strike), and the Climb action (and the die roll result you roll for the Athletics check to do so); you do not record your Reflex save against the aura’s effects. Preserve the Moment works best, as a result, when the actions you take are relatively standard and when rolls associated with those are high rolls.

You gain the following reaction, which you can use once during the next 24 hours. 

<document level="2" id="action-3533" />
name: Preserve the Moment

**Source** [Pathfinder Adventure Path #219: Lord of the Trinity Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=283) pg. 83 **Archetype** [Timewracked](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=340) **Prerequisites** [Timewracked Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8166) --- Your ability to interact with and even observe the flow of time allows you to fracture it slightly in a localized place whenever you land a particularly solid blow on a foe. Whenever you achieve a critical success with a Strike, the target of that Strike must also attempt a Will save against your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected by the fractured timeflow.
**Success** The target is [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 for 1 round.
**Failure** The target is slowed 2 for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The target is [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 3, then slowed 2 for 1 round after they recover from being stunned.
name: Fracture Time Flow

**Source** [Pathfinder Adventure Path #219: Lord of the Trinity Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=283) pg. 83 **Archetype** [Timewracked](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=340) **Prerequisites** [Timewracked Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8166) **Trigger** A creature targets you with a single-target spell or ability that you defend against with a saving throw or your AC. --- You reflexively attempt to cycle a creature’s timeline briefly to a reality where it’s less able to succeed. Spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3323). The creature that targets you must then attempt a Will save against your class DC or spell DC at mythic proficiency, whichever is higher. **Critical Success** The spell or ability affects you normally.
**Success** The creature’s attempt to target you is slightly hindered. If your AC is being targeted, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to your AC. If you need to attempt a saving throw, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to the save.
**Failure** The creature’s attempt to target you is significantly hindered. If your AC is being targeted, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to your AC, and a critical hit against you becomes a normal hit. If you need to attempt a saving throw, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to the save, and if you critically fail, you fail instead.
**Critical Failure** The creature’s attempt to target you fails, and the actions it used to make the attempt are wasted.
name: Not This Time

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 20"
    pfs=""
>
[Timelessness](/Feats.aspx?ID=8177) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder Adventure Path #219: Lord of the Trinity Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=283) pg. 83

**Archetype** [Timewracked](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=340)

**Prerequisites**
[Timewracked Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8166)

</column>

---

Time travelers are rare, but even among them your isolation from time is unique. As long as you remain timewracked, you do not age and cannot die of natural causes. You’re immune to hostile spells and effects related to time or aging, as well as the [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) condition (except for when you gain the slowed condition through this mythic destiny). You reduce the DC of recovery checks by 1. Whenever you roll a flat check to end persistent damage, you roll twice and choose your desired result; this is a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612) effect. In addition, your intimate knowledge of the flow of time gives you a measure of influence over the attempt of another to manipulate it. Whenever an enemy creature that you are aware of and who is within 60 feet of you gains the [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89) condition, you gain the quickened condition for 1 round. The actions available to you from the acquired condition are the same as those granted by the ability that quickens your enemy.
name: Timelessness

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 12"
    pfs=""
>
[Warshard Warrior Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8178) <actions string="" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Revenge of the Runelords</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Destiny" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=838" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder Adventure Path #219: Lord of the Trinity Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=283) pg. 84

**Archetype** [Warshard Warrior](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=341)

</column>

---

When you became infused with a shard of the god of war, you knew in your gut that your destiny would be one for the ages—yet you may not, at first, know the exact way such a destiny would play out. If you still carry the warshard that empowered you, you might carry it as a token or wear it as jewelry—if indeed it’s not simply embedded into your body. Regardless, the warshard itself no longer has any intrinsic power of its own, and even if you toss the warshard aside, its gifts do not leave you, nor can another benefit from them. If you throw the warshard away or lose it, your destiny remains unaltered; you have, in effect, become the warshard itself. This power manifests through a weapon you wield.

When you make your daily preparations, choose one weapon in your possession in which you have at least expert proficiency. That weapon becomes your warshard weapon until you choose a different weapon in your possession during another of your daily preparations. You can choose a mundane nonmagical weapon, a weapon with magical runes, one made of an unusual material, or even a specific magic weapon such as a [storm flash](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2873) or a [bloodletting kukri](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2862), but not an intelligent weapon or an artifact like the bastard sword [Serithtial](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3123) or one of the seven swords of sin. While you don’t lose access to your warshard warrior feats if your warshard weapon is taken from you or destroyed, note that several of this destiny’s feats require you to be wielding your warshard weapon. You can etch or remove runes from a warshard weapon normally. You can have only one warshard weapon at a time.

Your gain the [Mythic Strike](/Feats.aspx?ID=7302) feat, but you can only use it with your warshard weapon.

Whenever you make a Strike with your warshard weapon at mythic proficiency, you double the amount of additional damage you deal with the weapon specialization class feature. When you make Crafting checks to Repair your warshard weapon, your treat your proficiency rank in Crafting as legendary for determining how many Hit Points you restore to the item; in addition, if your Crafting result is a critical failure, you get a failure instead.

You also gain the following action. 

<document level="2" id="action-3534" />

<title level="2">Warshard Warrior Dedication leads to...</title>
[Athlete's Ally](/Feats.aspx?ID=8179), [Divide and Conquer](/Feats.aspx?ID=8184), [Final Shot Knows the Way](/Feats.aspx?ID=8187), [I Am the Weapon](/Feats.aspx?ID=8189), [Indomitable Shot](/Feats.aspx?ID=8185), [Lightless Sight](/Feats.aspx?ID=8180), [Prescient Parry](/Feats.aspx?ID=8186), [Steadfast Grip](/Feats.aspx?ID=8181), [Throw and Catch](/Feats.aspx?ID=8188), [Transmute Weapon](/Feats.aspx?ID=8182), [Warshard Rune](/Feats.aspx?ID=8183)
name: Warshard Warrior Dedication

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />

</column>

---

You channel the power of your warshard through your weapon to better perform athletic maneuvers. Spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3323). For 1 minute, when you attempt Athletics checks to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=2383), [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376), [Reposition](/Actions.aspx?ID=2379), [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380), or [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=2382) with your warshard weapon, you do so at mythic proficiency. Note that this ability doesn’t grant additional weapon traits to your weapon, so your options for which of these maneuvers you can attempt might be limited by your weapon’s existing traits.
name: Athlete's Ally

<traits>

Your DCs against attempts made to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=2383), [Steal](/Actions.aspx?ID=2410), or otherwise remove your warshard weapon from your possession are calculated at mythic proficiency. When you designate a warshard weapon, its [Hardness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2183) increases by an amount equal to half your level, and its Hit Points increase by an amount equal to your level for as long as it is your warshard weapon.

The material and quality of the material you select must be of a level equal to or less than your character level. You can transmute the weapon into a standard-grade item of that material only if the weapon is at least level 9. You can transmute the weapon into high-grade item of that material only if the weapon is at least level 15.

---

When you choose a warshard weapon during your daily preparations, you can empower it with an additional magical effect. Choose one common weapon [property rune](/Rules.aspx?ID=3168) that’s of a level equal to or lower than your level. That rune is applied to your warshard weapon as if it were etched onto it normally, but this weapon property does not count against the normal maximum number of property runes a weapon can have. If you empower a weapon with a property rune of the same type, only the highest-level one applies. This additional rune functions only when you wield your warshard weapon and have at least 1 unspent Mythic Point; otherwise, the rune becomes dormant until these conditions are met.
name: Warshard Rune
